name: planner
content:
**Plan:**

- Step 1:
      * sub-task: Data loading, preprocessing, and core computations (MSD, VACF, residence times, displacement PDFs, ergodicity breaking)
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load both datasets from their exact paths
        - For each N configuration (5, 10, 20, 40) and each Lévy walk β class (1.2, 1.5, 1.8, 2.5), extract trajectories using `x_true`/`y_true`
        - Apply Savitzky-Golay filter (window=7) to `x_noisy`/`y_noisy` for N=20 and N=40 only
        - Compute ensemble-averaged MSD vs lag time for all configurations; fit power-law slopes (α_emp) over the intermediate lag range (avoid very short and very long lags); print α_emp per configuration
        - Compute normalized VACF for each configuration; find zero-crossing lag times; print them
        - Compute instantaneous speeds, define "trapped" state as speed below 25th percentile per N group, compute residence time distributions for N=10, 20, 40; fit power-law tails; print γ ± uncertainty
        - Compute displacement increment PDFs (Δx) at lag steps 1, 5, 20, 50 for N=40 point-vortex and β=1.5 Lévy walk
        - Compute ergodicity-breaking parameter EB(Δt) = Var(TAMSD) / Mean(TAMSD)² for all configurations; print plateau values (mean over last 20% of lags)
        - Save all computed arrays (MSD curves, VACF, residence time data, displacement PDFs, EB curves, fitted parameters) to the output data folder as numpy/npz files for use in the plotting step

- Step 2:
      * sub-task: Generate all figures (Fig 1–7) from saved computation results
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load all precomputed arrays from the output data folder
        - **Fig 1**: 2×4 panel figure — top row: 2D tracer trajectories (x vs y) for N=5,10,20,40 colored by time (viridis); bottom row: 4 Lévy walk trajectories (β=1.2,1.5,1.8,2.5) colored by time; title per panel; save as `fig1_trajectories_<N>_<timestamp>.png`
        - **Fig 2**: Two-panel log-log MSD vs lag time — left: 4 vortex configs with fitted α_emp annotated; right: 4 Lévy walk classes with theoretical α=3-β dashed reference; add α=1 and α=2 reference lines; save as `fig2_msd_scaling_<N>_<timestamp>.png`
        - **Fig 3**: Two-panel VACF vs lag time (log x-axis) — left: point-vortex configs; right: Lévy walk classes; horizontal dashed at C_v=0; annotate zero-crossing times; save as `fig3_vacf_<N>_<timestamp>.png`
        - **Fig 4**: Single log-log panel — residence time distributions for N=10,20,40 with fitted power-law tails annotated; mark exponential cutoff for N=10; save as `fig4_residence_times_<N>_<timestamp>.png`
        - **Fig 5**: Two-panel displacement PDFs at 4 lag times — left: N=40 point-vortex; right: β=1.5 Lévy walk; overlay Gaussian (dashed) and Lévy-stable fit (dotted) at largest lag; log-log axes; save as `fig5_displacement_pdfs_<N>_<timestamp>.png`
        - **Fig 6**: Two-panel EB(Δt) vs Δt (log-log) — left: point-vortex; right: Lévy walks; save as `fig6_ergodicity_<N>_<timestamp>.png`
        - **Fig 7**: Regime map scatter — x: EB plateau, y: α_emp; circles for vortex configs (color=N), squares for Lévy walks (color=β); annotate each point; reference lines α=1, α=2, EB=0; save as `fig7_regime_map_<N>_<timestamp>.png`
        - Print all figure save paths

- Step 3:
      * sub-task: Write the comprehensive Results section
      * agent: researcher
      * bullet points:
        - Write a full Results section (minimum 1500 words) covering all 7 figures and their physical interpretation
        - Section 1: Tracer trajectory morphology from Fig 1 — describe visual differences between N=5 (weakly chaotic, compact) and N=40 (strongly chaotic, space-filling), and compare with Lévy walk trajectory morphology
        - Section 2: MSD scaling analysis from Fig 2 — report α_emp values per N configuration, discuss ballistic vs superdiffusive vs normal regimes, describe the N=40 transition, compare with Lévy walk theoretical α values
        - Section 3: VACF and Hamiltonian memory from Fig 3 — discuss anti-correlations, zero-crossing times, and what they imply about memory in the vortex system vs Lévy walks
        - Section 4: Residence time distributions from Fig 4 — report γ values, discuss power-law tails as mechanistic evidence for Lévy-like trapping, compare N=10 vs N=40
        - Section 5: Displacement PDF evolution from Fig 5 — describe transition from Gaussian to heavy-tailed distributions with increasing lag time, compare N=40 vortex vs β=1.5 Lévy walk
        - Section 6: Ergodicity breaking from Fig 6 — report EB plateau values, discuss quenched heterogeneity in vortex systems vs intrinsic Lévy non-ergodicity
        - Section 7: Regime map synthesis from Fig 7 — describe where each vortex configuration sits relative to Lévy walk ground truth, identify the effective Lévy-equivalence mapping
        - Section 8: Physical interpretation — connect results to the Kirchhoff-Onsager Hamiltonian, 1/r kernel, and effective fractional diffusion theory
        - Reference all figures by their saved filenames
        - Include all key numerical results (α_emp, γ, EB plateau, zero-crossing times) quoted from the printed outputs of Steps 1 and 2

name: planner_response_evaluator
content:
**PLAN**

- Step 1:
	* sub-task: Data loading, preprocessing, and core computations (MSD, VACF, residence times, displacement PDFs, ergodicity breaking)
	* agent in charge: engineer

	* instructions:
		- Load both datasets from their exact paths
		- For each N configuration (5, 10, 20, 40) and each Lévy walk β class (1.2, 1.5, 1.8, 2.5), extract trajectories using x_true/y_true
		- Apply Savitzky-Golay filter (window=7) to x_noisy/y_noisy for N=20 and N=40 only
		- Compute ensemble-averaged MSD vs lag time for all configurations; fit power-law slopes (α_emp) over the intermediate lag range (avoid very short and very long lags); print α_emp per configuration
		- Compute normalized VACF for each configuration; find zero-crossing lag times; print them
		- Compute instantaneous speeds, define "trapped" state as speed below 25th percentile per N group, compute residence time distributions for N=10, 20, 40; fit power-law tails; print γ ± uncertainty
		- Compute displacement increment PDFs (Δx) at lag steps 1, 5, 20, 50 for N=40 point-vortex and β=1.5 Lévy walk
		- Compute ergodicity-breaking parameter EB(Δt) = Var(TAMSD) / Mean(TAMSD)² for all configurations; print plateau values (mean over last 20% of lags)
		- Save all computed arrays (MSD curves, VACF, residence time data, displacement PDFs, EB curves, fitted parameters) to the output data folder as numpy/npz files for use in the plotting step

- Step 2:
	* sub-task: Generate all figures (Fig 1–7) from saved computation results
	* agent in charge: engineer

	* instructions:
		- Load all precomputed arrays from the output data folder
		- Fig 1: 2×4 panel figure — top row: 2D tracer trajectories (x vs y) for N=5,10,20,40 colored by time (viridis); bottom row: 4 Lévy walk trajectories (β=1.2,1.5,1.8,2.5) colored by time; title per panel; save as fig1_trajectories_<N>_<timestamp>.png
		- Fig 2: Two-panel log-log MSD vs lag time — left: 4 vortex configs with fitted α_emp annotated; right: 4 Lévy walk classes with theoretical α=3-β dashed reference; add α=1 and α=2 reference lines; save as fig2_msd_scaling_<N>_<timestamp>.png
		- Fig 3: Two-panel VACF vs lag time (log x-axis) — left: point-vortex configs; right: Lévy walk classes; horizontal dashed at C_v=0; annotate zero-crossing times; save as fig3_vacf_<N>_<timestamp>.png
		- Fig 4: Single log-log panel — residence time distributions for N=10,20,40 with fitted power-law tails annotated; mark exponential cutoff for N=10; save as fig4_residence_times_<N>_<timestamp>.png
		- Fig 5: Two-panel displacement PDFs at 4 lag times — left: N=40 point-vortex; right: β=1.5 Lévy walk; overlay Gaussian (dashed) and Lévy-stable fit (dotted) at largest lag; log-log axes; save as fig5_displacement_pdfs_<N>_<timestamp>.png
		- Fig 6: Two-panel EB(Δt) vs Δt (log-log) — left: point-vortex; right: Lévy walks; save as fig6_ergodicity_<N>_<timestamp>.png
		- Fig 7: Regime map scatter — x: EB plateau, y: α_emp; circles for vortex configs (color=N), squares for Lévy walks (color=β); annotate each point; reference lines α=1, α=2, EB=0; save as fig7_regime_map_<N>_<timestamp>.png
		- Print all figure save paths

- Step 3:
	* sub-task: Write the comprehensive Results section
	* agent in charge: researcher

	* instructions:
		- Write a full Results section (minimum 1500 words) covering all 7 figures and their physical interpretation
		- Section 1: Tracer trajectory morphology from Fig 1 — describe visual differences between N=5 (weakly chaotic, compact) and N=40 (strongly chaotic, space-filling), and compare with Lévy walk trajectory morphology
		- Section 2: MSD scaling analysis from Fig 2 — report α_emp values per N configuration, discuss ballistic vs superdiffusive vs normal regimes, describe the N=40 transition, compare with Lévy walk theoretical α values
		- Section 3: VACF and Hamiltonian memory from Fig 3 — discuss anti-correlations, zero-crossing times, and what they imply about memory in the vortex system vs Lévy walks
		- Section 4: Residence time distributions from Fig 4 — report γ values, discuss power-law tails as mechanistic evidence for Lévy-like trapping, compare N=10 vs N=40
		- Section 5: Displacement PDF evolution from Fig 5 — describe transition from Gaussian to heavy-tailed distributions with increasing lag time, compare N=40 vortex vs β=1.5 Lévy walk
		- Section 6: Ergodicity breaking from Fig 6 — report EB plateau values, discuss quenched heterogeneity in vortex systems vs intrinsic Lévy non-ergodicity
		- Section 7: Regime map synthesis from Fig 7 — describe where each vortex configuration sits relative to Lévy walk ground truth, identify the effective Lévy-equivalence mapping
		- Section 8: Physical interpretation — connect results to the Kirchhoff-Onsager Hamiltonian, 1/r kernel, and effective fractional diffusion theory
		- Reference all figures by their saved filenames
		- Include all key numerical results (α_emp, γ, EB plateau, zero-crossing times) quoted from the printed outputs of Steps 1 and 2

name: plan_reviewer
content:
**Recommendations:**

- In Step 1, the MSD fitting range should be explicitly specified as a fraction of the total lag range (e.g., fit over lags from 10% to 60% of the maximum lag) to avoid ambiguity in what "intermediate lag range" means, and the engineer should print both the fitted slope and the R² goodness-of-fit for each configuration to allow the researcher to assess reliability of the power-law fits.

- In Step 1, the VACF computation should clarify that velocities are computed as finite differences of x_true/y_true positions (not from noisy positions), and that the VACF should be computed per-trajectory first and then ensemble-averaged, to avoid mixing trajectory-specific memory effects; the zero-crossing should be found by linear interpolation between the last positive and first negative VACF value.

- In Step 1, the residence time distribution fitting should use a maximum-likelihood estimator (MLE) for the power-law tail exponent rather than a least-squares log-log fit, since log-log regression on binned histograms produces biased estimates; the uncertainty should be reported as the standard error from the MLE Fisher information, and the cutoff for the tail fit should be set at the 75th percentile of residence times to focus on the tail.

- In Step 1, the displacement increment PDFs should be computed as absolute displacements sqrt(Δx² + Δy²) in addition to the marginal Δx distributions, since the 2D radial displacement PDF is more directly comparable to the Lévy-stable radial distribution and avoids sign cancellation artifacts.

- In Step 1, the ergodicity-breaking parameter computation should explicitly handle the case where only 5 tracers are available per N configuration (point-vortex data), which gives very noisy EB estimates; the engineer should print a warning if fewer than 10 trajectories are used and note this limitation in the printed output.

- In Step 2, Fig 1 should use one representative trajectory per configuration (not all 5 tracers overlaid), selecting the trajectory with median total displacement to avoid showing an outlier; the colorbar should be shared across all panels in each row and labeled with time in seconds.

- In Step 2, Fig 2 should include error bands (shaded region = ±1 standard deviation across tracers) around the ensemble-averaged MSD curves for the point-vortex data, since with only 5 tracers per configuration the variance is large and the uncertainty on α_emp is significant; the fitted power-law line should be shown as a separate dashed line overlaid on the data.

- In Step 2, Fig 5 should use absolute displacement magnitude (radial PDF) rather than marginal Δx for the log-log displacement PDF panels, and the Lévy-stable fit at the largest lag time should report the fitted stability index α_stable and scale parameter in the panel annotation, not just overlay the curve.

- In Step 2, Fig 7 (regime map) should also include a color-coded arrow or line connecting each point-vortex configuration to its nearest Lévy walk equivalent (by Euclidean distance in the EB-α plane), making the effective Lévy-equivalence mapping visually explicit rather than requiring the reader to infer it from proximity.

- In Step 3, the researcher should explicitly tabulate all key numerical results (α_emp per N, γ per N, EB plateau per configuration, zero-crossing lag per configuration) in a markdown table within the Results section, rather than only embedding them in prose, to make the quantitative comparisons between point-vortex and Lévy walk datasets immediately readable.

name: reviewer_response_evaluator
content:
**Recommendations:**

In Step 1, the MSD fitting range should be explicitly specified as a fraction of the total lag range (e.g., fit over lags from 10% to 60% of the maximum lag) to avoid ambiguity in what intermediate lag range means, and the engineer should print both the fitted slope and the R² goodness-of-fit for each configuration to allow the researcher to assess reliability of the power-law fits.

In Step 1, the VACF computation should clarify that velocities are computed as finite differences of x_true/y_true positions (not from noisy positions), and that the VACF should be computed per-trajectory first and then ensemble-averaged, to avoid mixing trajectory-specific memory effects; the zero-crossing should be found by linear interpolation between the last positive and first negative VACF value.

In Step 1, the residence time distribution fitting should use a maximum-likelihood estimator (MLE) for the power-law tail exponent rather than a least-squares log-log fit, since log-log regression on binned histograms produces biased estimates; the uncertainty should be reported as the standard error from the MLE Fisher information, and the cutoff for the tail fit should be set at the 75th percentile of residence times to focus on the tail.

In Step 1, the displacement increment PDFs should be computed as absolute displacements sqrt(Δx² + Δy²) in addition to the marginal Δx distributions, since the 2D radial displacement PDF is more directly comparable to the Lévy-stable radial distribution and avoids sign cancellation artifacts.

In Step 1, the ergodicity-breaking parameter computation should explicitly handle the case where only 5 tracers are available per N configuration (point-vortex data), which gives very noisy EB estimates; the engineer should print a warning if fewer than 10 trajectories are used and note this limitation in the printed output.

In Step 2, Fig 1 should use one representative trajectory per configuration (not all 5 tracers overlaid), selecting the trajectory with median total displacement to avoid showing an outlier; the colorbar should be shared across all panels in each row and labeled with time in seconds.

In Step 2, Fig 2 should include error bands (shaded region = ±1 standard deviation across tracers) around the ensemble-averaged MSD curves for the point-vortex data, since with only 5 tracers per configuration the variance is large and the uncertainty on α_emp is significant; the fitted power-law line should be shown as a separate dashed line overlaid on the data.

In Step 2, Fig 5 should use absolute displacement magnitude (radial PDF) rather than marginal Δx for the log-log displacement PDF panels, and the Lévy-stable fit at the largest lag time should report the fitted stability index α_stable and scale parameter in the panel annotation, not just overlay the curve.

In Step 2, Fig 7 (regime map) should also include a color-coded arrow or line connecting each point-vortex configuration to its nearest Lévy walk equivalent (by Euclidean distance in the EB-α plane), making the effective Lévy-equivalence mapping visually explicit rather than requiring the reader to infer it from proximity.

In Step 3, the researcher should explicitly tabulate all key numerical results (α_emp per N, γ per N, EB plateau per configuration, zero-crossing lag per configuration) in a markdown table within the Results section, rather than only embedding them in prose, to make the quantitative comparisons between point-vortex and Lévy walk datasets immediately readable.

name: review_recorder
content:
Recommendations have been logged.
Number of feedback rounds left: 0.
Now, update the plan accordingly, planner!

name: planner
content:
**Plan:**

- Step 1:
      * sub-task: Data loading, preprocessing, and all core computations
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load both datasets from their exact absolute paths (`/home/node/work/projects/superdiffusion_v2/point_vortex_tracers.npy` and `/home/node/work/projects/superdiffusion_v2/levy_walk_trajectories.npy`)
        - Extract trajectories per N configuration (5, 10, 20, 40) and per Lévy walk β class (1.2, 1.5, 1.8, 2.5) using `x_true`/`y_true` fields only for all mechanistic analyses
        - Apply Savitzky-Golay filter (window=7) to `x_noisy`/`y_noisy` for N=20 and N=40 only; store filtered positions separately
        - **MSD**: Compute ensemble-averaged MSD and per-trajectory MSD vs lag time for all configurations; fit power-law slope α_emp over lags from 10% to 60% of maximum lag using log-log linear regression; print α_emp and R² per configuration
        - **VACF**: Compute velocities as finite differences of `x_true`/`y_true`; compute VACF per trajectory first, then ensemble-average; normalize by zero-lag value; find zero-crossing by linear interpolation between last positive and first negative value; print zero-crossing lag per configuration
        - **Residence times**: Compute instantaneous speeds from `x_true`/`y_true` finite differences; define trapped state as speed below 25th percentile per N group; compute residence time distributions for N=10, 20, 40; fit power-law tail exponent γ using MLE (Hill estimator) above the 75th percentile of residence times; report γ ± standard error from Fisher information; print results
        - **Displacement PDFs**: Compute both marginal Δx and absolute radial displacement sqrt(Δx²+Δy²) at lag steps 1, 5, 20, 50 for N=40 point-vortex and β=1.5 Lévy walk
        - **Ergodicity breaking**: Compute TAMSD per trajectory and EB(Δt) = Var(TAMSD)/Mean(TAMSD)² for all configurations; print warning if fewer than 10 trajectories are used (point-vortex case); print EB plateau (mean over last 20% of lags) per configuration
        - Save all computed arrays (MSD curves, per-trajectory MSDs, VACF curves, residence time data, displacement PDF histograms, EB curves, all fitted parameters) as npz files to the output data folder

- Step 2:
      * sub-task: Generate all 7 publication-quality figures from saved computation results
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load all precomputed arrays from the output data folder
        - **Fig 1** (2×4 panels): Top row — one representative 2D trajectory per vortex config (N=5,10,20,40), selected as the trajectory with median total displacement; bottom row — one representative trajectory per Lévy walk β class (1.2,1.5,1.8,2.5); color each by time using viridis; shared colorbar per row labeled "Time (s)"; title per panel showing N or β; save as `fig1_trajectories_<timestamp>.png`
        - **Fig 2** (2 panels): Left — log-log ensemble-averaged MSD vs lag time for all 4 vortex configs with ±1 std shaded bands, fitted power-law dashed line overlaid, α_emp annotated; right — log-log MSD for all 4 Lévy walk classes with theoretical α=3-β dashed reference lines; add α=1 and α=2 reference lines in both panels; save as `fig2_msd_scaling_<timestamp>.png`
        - **Fig 3** (2 panels): Left — VACF vs lag time (log x-axis) for all vortex configs; right — VACF for all Lévy walk classes; horizontal dashed at C_v=0; annotate zero-crossing times; save as `fig3_vacf_<timestamp>.png`
        - **Fig 4** (1 panel): Log-log residence time distributions for N=10, 20, 40 with fitted power-law tails annotated with γ values; mark exponential cutoff for N=10 with vertical dashed line; save as `fig4_residence_times_<timestamp>.png`
        - **Fig 5** (2 panels): Use absolute radial displacement magnitude PDFs at 4 lag times; left — N=40 point-vortex; right — β=1.5 Lévy walk; overlay Gaussian reference (dashed) and Lévy-stable fit (dotted) at largest lag time with fitted stability index α_stable and scale parameter annotated in panel; log-log axes; save as `fig5_displacement_pdfs_<timestamp>.png`
        - **Fig 6** (2 panels): Left — EB(Δt) vs Δt (log-log) for all vortex configs; right — EB(Δt) for all Lévy walk classes; save as `fig6_ergodicity_<timestamp>.png`
        - **Fig 7** (1 panel): Regime map scatter — x: EB plateau, y: α_emp; circles for vortex configs (color-coded by N), squares for Lévy walks (color-coded by β); annotate each point with its label; draw color-coded arrows connecting each vortex config to its nearest Lévy walk equivalent by Euclidean distance in the EB-α plane; reference lines α=1, α=2 (horizontal dashed) and EB=0 (vertical dotted); save as `fig7_regime_map_<timestamp>.png`
        - Print all figure save paths

- Step 3:
      * sub-task: Write the comprehensive Results section
      * agent: researcher
      * bullet points:
        - Write a full Results section (minimum 1500 words) in academic style covering all 7 figures
        - **Section 1 — Trajectory morphology**: Describe visual differences between N=5 (compact, weakly chaotic) and N=40 (space-filling, strongly chaotic) from Fig 1; compare with Lévy walk trajectory morphology (long straight flights vs tortuous vortex paths)
        - **Section 2 — MSD scaling**: Report all α_emp values and R² per N configuration from Fig 2; discuss ballistic vs superdiffusive vs normal regimes; describe the N=40 transition; compare empirical α_emp with Lévy walk theoretical α=3-β values
        - **Section 3 — VACF and memory**: Report zero-crossing lag times per configuration from Fig 3; discuss anti-correlations and what they imply about Hamiltonian memory in the vortex system vs Lévy walks
        - **Section 4 — Residence times**: Report γ ± uncertainty per N from Fig 4; discuss power-law tails as mechanistic evidence for Lévy-like trapping; compare N=10 vs N=40 tail exponents
        - **Section 5 — Displacement PDF evolution**: Describe transition from Gaussian to heavy-tailed distributions with increasing lag time from Fig 5; compare N=40 vortex vs β=1.5 Lévy walk; report fitted α_stable and scale parameter
        - **Section 6 — Ergodicity breaking**: Report EB plateau values per configuration from Fig 6; discuss quenched heterogeneity in vortex systems vs intrinsic Lévy non-ergodicity; note the limitation of only 5 tracers per vortex config
        - **Section 7 — Regime map**: Describe where each vortex configuration sits relative to Lévy walk ground truth from Fig 7; identify the effective Lévy-equivalence mapping (which β class best matches each N)
        - **Section 8 — Physical interpretation**: Connect results to the Kirchhoff-Onsager Hamiltonian, 1/r kernel, and effective fractional diffusion theory
        - Include a markdown table summarizing all key numerical results: α_emp per N, γ per N, EB plateau per configuration, zero-crossing lag per configuration, and nearest Lévy walk equivalent per N
        - Reference all figures by their exact saved filenames as reported in Step 2 output

		- Section 7 — Regime map: Describe where each vortex configuration sits relative to Lévy walk ground truth from Fig 7; identify the effective Lévy-equivalence mapping (which β class best matches each N)
		- Section 8 — Physical interpretation: Connect results to the Kirchhoff-Onsager Hamiltonian, 1/r kernel, and effective fractional diffusion theory
		- Include a markdown table summarizing all key numerical results: α_emp per N, γ per N, EB plateau per configuration, zero-crossing lag per configuration, and nearest Lévy walk equivalent per N
		- Reference all figures by their exact saved filenames as reported in Step 2 output
